title: Flowise CSV Agent RCE via bypassable Python import allow-regex before Pyodide execution
description: The CSV Agent node (packages/components/nodes/agents/CSVAgent/CSVAgent.ts) builds a system prompt from the user's question, asks the configured LLM to emit pandas Python code, and then evaluates that LLM-generated code with pyodide.runPythonAsync() in a non-sandboxed Pyodide environment. Before execution the code is filtered by validatePythonCodeForDataFrame() in packages/components/src/pythonCodeValidator.ts, whose FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS uses the regex /\bimport\s+(?!pandas|numpy\b)/g intending to allow only pandas/numpy imports. That negative-lookahead only inspects the first imported name, so a statement like `import pandas as np, os as pandas` passes validation while still importing the os module (aliased as pandas), after which `pandas.system("...")` runs arbitrary OS commands. An unauthenticated attacker who can submit a prompt to a chatflow using the CSV Agent node (or an authenticated attacker who points the node at an attacker-controlled model server) can use prompt injection to obtain such code and achieve remote code execution on the Flowise server.
